Hey, what's going on everyone? Okay, the lead generation overview. I probably should have started here with a nice little branding. There it is, Gym Launch, aka Rainmaking for LeadGent. So this is the overview and there's two parts. One of them will be the user journey and the other will be the actual infrastructure part, just kind of overview and then we'll get into the nitty gritty of building it. So the user journey is fairly straightforward. They don't see all the stuff that's happening in the background to optimize this process. But essentially they're going to see an ad on whatever platform we're using. For right now, most people are running from Facebook ads. So they'll be seeing an ad on social media. They will click the ad. That click will take them to a landing page with the intention of capturing information in the form of an opt-in. So that's how we get their name, phone number, email. And then after the opt-in, we will take them to a thank you page. And on that thank you page, they will have the ability to schedule their appointment and that cuts down on some of the communication that you need to have with them so that you don't have to say, when are you available? When are you free? I'm free at five. But you don't have to do all that. It's already scheduled. And then at that point, the rest of our attention is getting them to show and confirming the appointment, et cetera. After they schedule, they will be routed to your Facebook business page so that they can know that you are a legitimate business and not a scam. So let's walk through this. So here's an example of one of the gems that has been with us. This is Braini Shimp Feed Your Soul. This is an example of an ad that you might run. We'll get into the nitty-gritty of how to make these ads later. But the prospect will click this ad. They'd be taken to a landing page. We'll show you how to make this. They would say, wow, this looks interesting. I want in. Mind you, the page and the ads and the copy, all these things are continually dynamic as in they are changing. So do not get married to this particular landing page. We're always producing new ones that are converting at higher rates. So in the training, if you see a different funnel or different ads or different copy or creative, don't be alarmed. It means that you are part of a dynamic program that is always growing and getting better. So let's see here. We're going to go Alex. Test, test, test. Oops. I probably need to put a... Brandy, sorry about this. All right. Cool. Let's see. Ready for a change. And then they will have the opportunity to pick their time. Brandy does large groups, which I do not recommend, but she's a legacy person. So she's been working with us for over a year and a half. So she is more experienced in large group sales. If you are starting out, which you probably are for watching this video, I recommend doing one-on-one sales and we will show you how to structure these schedules so that you can optimally get as many people to schedule and show. So anyways, you'd pick a time, continue, and then you can see we already have code inside of here that automatically populates this so they don't have to enter it in again, complete the appointment, and then you can add it to the calendar. And then that is it. Very straightforward. That's the process on the user experience side.
